api/v3/filters.purpose: Provides AIP-style filter[field][op]=value query-parameter parsing and conversion into the pkg/filter domain filter types consumed by service List methods. Three concerns: filter.go defines API-layer filter structs, parse.go reflection-parses url.Values into them, convert.go maps them to pkg/filter.

patterns.name: filters.Parse for URL to typed filter struct
patterns.description: Call filters.Parse(r.URL.Query(), &myFilter) in the request decoder. Target must be a non-nil pointer to a struct (or **struct for lazy allocation) whose fields are *FilterString, *FilterStringExact, *FilterULID, *FilterNumeric, *FilterDateTime, *FilterBoolean, FilterLabels, *string, or *NamedStringType. Field names come from json struct tags.
patterns.example:
var f MyFilters
if err := filters.Parse(r.URL.Query(), &f); err != nil { return err }

patterns.name: FromAPI* converters for API filter to pkg/filter
patterns.description: After parsing, convert each field via FromAPIFilterString/ULID/StringExact/Numeric/DateTime/Boolean/Labels. Each returns (nil, nil) when input is nil — callers must treat nil as 'no filter applied'. Multi-operator combinations (e.g. Gt+Lte) are normalized to And-of-single-op leaves.

patterns.name: filter[field][op]=value bracket convention
patterns.description: All filter params use bracket syntax (filter[name][eq]=foo, filter[created_at][gte]=2024-01-01T00:00:00Z). Bare filter[field]=value equals filter[field][eq]=value for string fields. Label fields use dot-notation filter[labels.env][eq]=prod or nested filter[labels][env][eq]=prod.

patterns.name: Unknown filter fields rejected by Parse
patterns.description: checkUnknownFilterKeys validates every filter[x] key against a json-tagged field of the target struct; unrecognised names return an error, enforcing a strict API contract.
patterns.example: // filter[typo][eq]=x -> error: unknown filter field(s): typo

patterns.name: FromAPIStatusFilter for enum status fields
patterns.description: Generic helper FromAPIStatusFilter[T validator](ctx, *FilterStringExact) accepts only eq and oeq, builds a []T, and validates each value via T.Validate(); neq returns an error.

patterns.name: Pointer-to-pointer target lazily allocates
patterns.description: If target is **MyFilters, Parse allocates the inner struct only when at least one filter key is present (hasFilterKeys); otherwise the pointer stays nil. Use for optional filter parameters.
patterns.example:
var f *MyFilters
if err := filters.Parse(r.URL.Query(), &f); err != nil { return err }

key_file_guides.role: API-layer filter struct definitions (FilterString, FilterStringExact, FilterULID, FilterNumeric, FilterDateTime, FilterBoolean, FilterLabel, FilterLabels). Add a new filter type here when pkg/filter gains a new base type.
key_file_guides.watch_for: FilterLabels is a type alias (map[string]FilterLabel), NOT a pointer-to-struct; it is special-cased in parseFiltersValue via filterLabelsType/filterLabelsPtrType reflection checks.

key_file_guides.role: Reflection-driven URL query parser dispatching on field type via reflect.TypeFor constants; handles *string, *NamedStringType (parseStringPtrTyped), and *T implementing encoding.TextUnmarshaler (parseTextUnmarshalerPtr).
key_file_guides.watch_for: FilterULIDType is exported (capital F) while sibling type vars are unexported — external callers reference it in switch statements. maxCommaSeparatedItems=50 and maxFilterValueLength=1024 are hard limits. *string/typed fields reject operator-style keys.

key_file_guides.role: Converts each API filter struct to its pkg/filter equivalent, canonicalizing multi-operator inputs into And-of-single-op leaf nodes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Every FromAPI* returns (nil, nil) on nil input. FilterStringExact maps to a single FilterString (no And split). FromAPIStatusFilter wraps validation errors in models.NewNillableGenericValidationError.

anti_patterns: Parsing filter query params manually with r.URL.Query().Get() instead of filters.Parse
Passing pkg/filter types directly as API response/wire fields — they are internal types
Adding a new operator string in parse.go without matching handling in convert.go
Treating a non-nil FromAPI* return for an empty filter as set — empty input returns nil
Using filterLabelsType reflection for anything other than the FilterLabels field

decisions.decision: Reflection-driven Parse dispatching on field type rather than per-field generated code
decisions.rationale: A single Parse handles all filter structs uniformly; adding a filterable field needs only a json-tagged struct field, no new parsing code.

decisions.decision: Multi-operator combinations normalized to And-of-leaves in FromAPI* converters
decisions.rationale: pkg/filter requires each node to carry at most one operator, so the converter canonicalizes ranges (Gte+Lte -> And[{Gte},{Lte}]) to always emit a valid filter tree that passes Validate().

code_examples.scenario: Parsing and converting filters in a v3 list handler decoder

api/v3/handlers/addons.purpose: v3 HTTP handlers for addon CRUD plus lifecycle (publish/archive), translating apiv3.Addon wire types to addon.Service domain calls through the httptransport pipeline. Each operation lives in its own file.

patterns.name: One operation per file
patterns.description: Each CRUD/lifecycle operation has its own file (create.go, delete.go, get.go, list.go, update.go, archive.go, publish.go). handler.go only declares the Handler interface and New() constructor.

patterns.name: Type-alias triad per operation
patterns.description: Every operation file declares <Op>Request = addon.<Op>Input, <Op>Response = apiv3.Type, and <Op>Handler = httptransport.Handler[Req,Resp] (or HandlerWithArgs for path-param ops).
patterns.example: type (ArchiveAddonRequest = addon.ArchiveAddonInput; ArchiveAddonResponse = apiv3.Addon; ArchiveAddonHandler httptransport.HandlerWithArgs[ArchiveAddonRequest, ArchiveAddonResponse, string])

patterns.name: resolveNamespace first in every decoder
patterns.description: Decoders call ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx) first and propagate the error before building the domain input.
patterns.example: ns, err := h.resolveNamespace(ctx); if err != nil { return ArchiveAddonRequest{}, err }

patterns.name: convert.go owns all domain<->API mapping
patterns.description: All ToAPI*/FromAPI* functions live in convert.go; operation files call them and never inline-convert. BillingPrice/RateCard discriminated unions are encoded manually via Discriminator()/From* calls.

patterns.name: Nil-check pointer results before conversion
patterns.description: Handlers receiving *addon.Addon guard against nil before ToAPIAddon, returning a descriptive error.
patterns.example: if a == nil { return ArchiveAddonResponse{}, fmt.Errorf("failed to archive add-on") }

patterns.name: IgnoreNonCriticalIssues on mutating inputs
patterns.description: Create and update decoders set req.IgnoreNonCriticalIssues = true after FromAPI conversion to suppress non-fatal validation issues.

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface listing all endpoint methods + handler struct (resolveNamespace func, service addon.Service, options []httptransport.HandlerOption) + New() constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New endpoint = add to the Handler interface here first, then create a dedicated file.

key_file_guides.role: All bidirectional apiv3<->productcatalog/addon mapping: rate cards, prices (flat/unit/tiered graduated+volume), tax config, discounts, labels, status.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Rate-card and price type switches are exhaustive — a new RateCardType or PriceType needs a case in both ToAPIBillingRateCard/ToAPIBillingPrice and FromAPIBillingRateCard. FlatFeeRateCard leaves BillingCadence nil; UsageBased sets it. Missing Price => free price.

key_file_guides.role: ArchiveAddon sets EffectiveTo: clock.Now() in the decoder — the HTTP layer owns wall-clock time.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Lifecycle ops (publish, archive) must set clock.Now() in the decoder, never inside the service.

key_file_guides.role: ListAddons with page pagination (pagination.NewPage defaults 1,20), validating page before building ListAddonsInput.
key_file_guides.watch_for: page.Validate errors must surface as apierrors.NewBadRequestError with Field='page'.

anti_patterns: Inline type conversion in operation files instead of calling ToAPIAddon/FromAPICreate*
Returning domain errors without the httptransport GenericErrorEncoder chain
Skipping h.resolveNamespace(ctx) in a new operation decoder
Using httptransport.NewHandler instead of NewHandlerWithArgs for path-param operations
Adding validation/defaults logic outside the domain service or convert.go

decisions.decision: Lifecycle operations set clock.Now() in the decoder, not the service
decisions.rationale: HTTP layer owns request-scoped wall-clock time; the domain service receives explicit timestamps, making it deterministic and testable.

decisions.decision: convert.go hand-codes BillingPrice/RateCard union encoding instead of goverter
decisions.rationale: BillingPrice is a oneOf requiring Discriminator()+From* calls goverter cannot generate; a manual switch is the only safe option.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new addon path-param lifecycle endpoint

api/v3/handlers/apps.purpose: v3 HTTP handlers for listing and getting installed billing apps (Stripe, Sandbox, CustomInvoicing), performing polymorphic type-assertion on app.App to map each concrete type onto the apiv3.BillingApp oneOf union.

patterns.name: Polymorphic app mapping via type-assertion switch
patterns.description: ToAPIBillingApp in convert.go switches on item.GetType(), type-asserts to the concrete app (appstripe.App, appsandbox.App, appcustominvoicing.App), builds the per-type wire struct, then calls the matching From* on api.BillingApp.
patterns.example: billingApp := api.BillingApp{}; if err := billingApp.FromBillingAppStripe(billingAppStripe); err != nil { return billingApp, err }

patterns.name: Goverter for struct mappings, hand-code for unions
patterns.description: convert.go carries goverter:variables annotations generating convert.gen.go for simple mappings (PagePaginatedResponse, BillingAppCatalogItem, AppType enum); the polymorphic ToAPIBillingApp is hand-written and listed via goverter:extend.

patterns.name: handler struct holds app.Service
patterns.description: Unlike other handlers holding a domain-specific service, the apps handler holds the top-level app.Service registry.
patterns.example: type handler struct { resolveNamespace func(context.Context) (string, error); appService app.Service; options []httptransport.HandlerOption }

patterns.name: Compile-time interface check
patterns.description: handler.go includes var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil) to catch missing method implementations at compile time.

key_file_guides.role: Hand-written ToAPIBillingApp polymorphic switch + per-type helpers (toAPIBillingAppStripe/Sandbox/ExternalInvoicing) + goverter annotations for list/catalog/enum conversions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: goverter:enum:map annotations must stay in sync with app.AppType constants (else @error). A new app type needs a switch case AND a toAPIBillingApp<Type> helper. AppTypeCustomInvoicing maps to BillingAppTypeExternalInvoicing.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by goverter — DO NOT EDIT. Regenerate with go generate ./... after changing convert.go annotations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Manual edits are overwritten on the next generate; build tag is //go:build !goverter.

key_file_guides.role: Handler interface (ListApps, GetApp) + New() constructor with var _ Handler = (*handler)(nil) satisfaction check.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New app endpoints must be added to the Handler interface.

anti_patterns: Adding a new app type without a case in the ToAPIBillingApp switch
Editing convert.gen.go directly instead of regenerating
Accessing app-specific fields without first type-asserting to the concrete app type
Bypassing ToAPIBillingApp to inline-convert in get_app.go or list_app.go

decisions.decision: BillingApp oneOf mapping done via app.App interface -> concrete assertion -> From* call
decisions.rationale: The generated union requires the From* discriminator method; no generic field copy works across heterogeneous concrete types.

decisions.decision: Goverter only for struct-level mappings; the discriminated union is hand-coded
decisions.rationale: Goverter cannot handle interface type-switches or oneOf union encoding.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new billing app type to the polymorphic mapping
